review_comment:
This message should have been moved in #19649

I think Ok if we have not had reports of floating point exceptions since enabled.
the relevant bug also affects rmod, floordiv, and rfloordiv

have you got an example of floordiv, and rfloordiv failing.

```python
>>> pd.__version__
'1.1.2'
>>>
>>> from pandas.core.computation import expressions as expr
>>>
>>> expr.set_test_mode(True)
>>>
>>> s = pd.Series(np.arange(10001)) - 5000
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[True]
>>> result = s % 24
>>>
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[True]
>>>
>>> for i, elem in enumerate(s):
...     assert elem % 24 == result[i]
...
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 2, in <module>
AssertionError
>>>
>>> s = pd.Series(np.arange(10001)) - 5000
>>>
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[True]
>>> result = s % -24
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[True]
>>>
>>> for i, elem in enumerate(s):
...     assert elem % -24 == result[i]
...
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 2, in <module>
AssertionError
>>>
>>> s = pd.Series(np.arange(10001)) - 5000
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[True]
>>> s[s == 0] = 1
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[True]
>>> result = 24 % s
>>>
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[True]
>>>
>>> for i, elem in enumerate(s):
...     assert 24 % elem == result[i]
...
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 2, in <module>
AssertionError
>>>
>>>
>>> s = pd.Series(np.arange(10001)) - 5000
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[True]
>>>
>>> s[s == 0] = 1
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[True]
>>> result = -24 % s
>>>
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[True]
>>>
>>> for i, elem in enumerate(s):
...     assert -24 % elem == result[i]
...
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 2, in <module>
AssertionError
>>>
>>>
>>> s = pd.Series(np.arange(10001)) - 5000
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[True]
>>>
>>> result = s // 24
>>>
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[]
>>>
>>> for i, elem in enumerate(s):
...     assert elem // 24 == result[i]
...
>>>
>>> result = s // -24
>>>
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[]
>>>
>>> for i, elem in enumerate(s):
...     assert elem // -24 == result[i]
...
>>>
>>> s = pd.Series(np.arange(10001)) - 5000
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[True]
>>> s[s == 0] = 1
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[True]
>>> result = 24 // s
>>>
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[]
>>>
>>> for i, elem in enumerate(s):
...     assert 24 // elem == result[i]
...
>>>
>>> result = -24 // s
>>>
>>> print(expr.get_test_result())
[]
>>>
>>> for i, elem in enumerate(s):
...     assert -24 // elem == result[i]
...
>>>
```

> the relevant bug...

I wasn't planning a bug fix, just a simple regression fix with revert of offending code. could open directly against 1.1.x?
My bad, assumed it had the same behavior as cython without checking.

it may do. not checked. but from the output above, numexpr not being used for floordiv, rfloordiv.

Is there special handling for that?
In core.ops.array_ops.na_arithmetic_op we have a try/except for the numexpr call that catches TypeError.  Best guess is that floordiv _always_ gets caught here.
